title: AT Field metaphors
wikitext:
[]: AT Field metaphors             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Wed May 18, 2011 10:44 am I was reading through the commentary section and found someone's theory that Gendo's Glasses = his AT Field interesting. It seems to make sense - after all, when Rei breaks down his AT Field she takes his glasses away, and one of the only times he opens up to someone in anyway is when he saves Rei in the entry plug - incidentally at the same point that his glasses come off and a crack forms on them. This got me thinking about the other characters, and I noticed that quite a few could also have metaphors for AT Fields. So far I have: Misato - Beer. She drinks her problems away and I remember that she often raised her can of beer to avoid acknowledging someone/ something, or when faced with confrontation, effectively putting the beer between herself and the person/ situation. Asuka - Her A10 nerve connectors. Asuka more or less defines herself as an Eva Pilot, and believes that it makes her special, important; "Momma! Momma! I'm an elite pilot now!". She is also the only one of the pilots who never takes the headgear off, even when at home. Gendo - His glasses. Forming a literal barrier between him and other people. His glasses also seem to be the same shade of orange as actual AT Fields. Shinji - SDAT Player. Shinji runs away from Misato in one of the earlier episodes, and during this time he is almost constantly listening to music on his SDAT Player. In episode 23 it also seperates Misato and Shinji from each other when she makes advances, and when Misato puts her hand over it to touch him he turns away. The scene with Shinji and Asuka on the floor, however, may disprove this one. Shinji 2 - AT Field floor (Rug). The floor in Shinji's room has a strange, white marking on the floor; a strange, white marking identical to an AT Field. (Turns out it's a rug, my mistake). There are various scenes in which Misato comes to the door, trying to console or support Shinji, to no avail. In all these cases, these AT Field markings are between both of them. Rei - I thought perhaps Gendo's glasses, since one of the only times she shows explicit emotion is when Shinji wears them - effectively forcing his way past her "Sacred space which no one may enter" as Kaworu put it, thus offending Rei. She also has the glasses with her in Episode 19 when fighting Zeruel, when she says something to the effect of "Even if Ikari won't fight, I will" which I always saw as her disapproving of Shinji's decision to run away, arguably losing the connection she had with him and the glasses - her AT Field - return (Or did Rei always have them in the Eva?) . Rei taking Gendo's glasses for herself in EoE may also show how she has put a wall between her and Gendo, choosing Shinji instead. Ritsuko - Cats. I've yet to think of how this one works, and it may simply be related to Kaji and not this AT Field theory, but they're the only thing I can think of that could relate to this. So what do you think? Fan wank or something worth taking a closer look at?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed May 18, 2011 12:51 pm Shinji's rug is an AT Field, yes. From the Eva 2 video game: Somewhat blurry image from PSP version (I took the photo years ago). http://i74.photobucket.com/albums/i267/sailorstardust/Games/Shinjiroom03.jpg I need to think a little about these before giving my own answer, though.

[]: Re: AT Field metaphors             Quote Post by Stryker » Wed May 18, 2011 1:28 pm liquidus118 wrote: Shinji - SDAT Player.. The scene with Shinji and Asuka on the floor, however, may disprove this one.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed May 18, 2011 3:49 pm That'd be taking the metaphor too far - it's probably simply meant to be weird noise to create tension/the notion of a time-limit. Also, note that an AT-Field doesn't only bring about isolation, but (more importantly) individuality , our poetically dispositioned pretty boy went as far as calling it "The Light of the Soul". It's not an entirely negative thing, unlike retreat from the world via beer/electronic helpers - in the end, the right decision was to flip those things [the ATFs] back on, wasn't it? I'm not sure is 100%-ily equating the ATF to symbols of isolation is the right approach. The thing with the rug is interesting, tough - A person's own room is also something of a "sacred domain that belongs only to oneself", isn't it? And think of that dialogue from episode 26 that mentions Shinji's room (along with other stuff he owns) as his own borders, in both a good and a bad way - it is a place for his individuality (Stuff is gradually amassed in there) but he also uses it as a place to retreat from Misato.

[]: Re: AT Field metaphors             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Wed May 18, 2011 3:52 pm Stryker wrote: Shinji perhaps could have lowered his "A.T Field" (His SDAT having a spasm attack) down, letting him get close to Asuka/Asuka get close to him.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed May 18, 2011 6:52 pm Kendrix wrote: Also, note that an AT-Field doesn't only bring about isolation, but (more importantly) individuality , our poetically dispositioned pretty boy went as far as calling it "The Light of the Soul". It's not an entirely negative thing, unlike retreat from the world via beer/electronic helpers - in the end, the right decision was to flip those things [the ATFs] back on, wasn't it? I'm not sure is 100%-ily equating the ATF to symbols of isolation is the right approach.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed May 18, 2011 9:25 pm Azathoth wrote: Well that's the whole point of the story, isn't it? Isolation from others is the only thing that makes existence possible, and it also necessarily makes existence miserable.

[]:             Quote Post by juaxoo » Fri May 20, 2011 3:12 am AT field, anti-terror field, anti-terror can be translated as "confidence" a human isnt worth shit without confidence. and is incompetent and completely useless (this is stated by Shinji and how he describes himself at times) this is what AT field really means metaphorically. the end

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri May 20, 2011 3:17 am juaxoo wrote: AT field, anti-terror field, anti-terror can be translated as "confidence"

[]:             Quote Post by juaxoo » Fri May 20, 2011 3:20 am Ornette wrote: http://wiki.evageeks.org/FGC:OP_B#cut_OP_029

[]:             Quote Post by LoCooLaidman » Fri May 20, 2011 11:17 am I always thought that an A.T field was the projection of one's soul or some other matter-of-sort like that... But this isn't the topic for that! I'm not too fond of the cat one, but the rest makes sense... Gendo and his glasses seem to be the easist and most logical one to me. Shinji's SDAT player works really well for this too.
